conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

(This could include full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school but hasn't started anything else yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Since Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training, this is an important factor. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is generally payable for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

Before I make my final assessment, I need to ask one more question:

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning does he live with you, or do you contribute to his upkeep?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16 years old, or under 20 years old if they are in approved full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or Scottish Highers) or approved training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria.

**However**, if Alex were to start approved education or training in the future (before he turns 20), you could potentially become eligible for Child Benefit again at that point. If his circumstances change, it would be worth reapplying.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any education or training program, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. While the claimant is responsible for Alex (he lives at home), the fundamental age and education requirements are not satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives at home. However, Alex is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility extends only to children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Since Alex has turned 18 and is not enrolled in qualifying education or training, the claimant is not eligible to receive Child Benefit for him.
